title: How Chatbots Think

modules.title: The Robot That Talks Back
modules.sub: Meet the chatbot and discover what makes it speak
modules.description: Learners step inside the chatbot factory and have their first conversation with a talking machine, noticing what feels surprising or strange about its replies. They begin asking questions and forming hunches about what might be happening behind the scenes.

modules.title: Where Do the Words Come From?
modules.sub: Trace a chatbot's words back to patterns it learned
modules.description: Learners run simple experiments to see how a chatbot chooses its next word by looking for patterns, like finishing a familiar sentence in the most expected way. They play a guessing game that mimics what the bot is doing, building a gut-level feel for prediction.

modules.title: Teaching a Bot With Examples
modules.sub: See how reading lots of text shapes what a bot knows
modules.description: Learners act as the teacher, feeding the bot different sets of example sentences and watching how its answers change depending on what it has read. They make choices about which examples to include and start to understand that a bot only knows what it has been shown.

modules.title: When the Bot Gets Confused
modules.sub: Find out why bots say silly or wrong things sometimes
modules.description: Learners deliberately trip up the chatbot with tricky questions and unusual prompts, then investigate why it went wrong. They identify patterns in the mistakes and begin to explain which situations are most likely to fool a bot that is just predicting words.

modules.title: Give the Bot a Job to Do
modules.sub: Write instructions that help a chatbot do something useful
modules.description: Learners craft simple prompts to give the chatbot a specific task, like writing a short poem or answering a riddle, and compare how different instructions lead to very different results. They revise their prompts based on what works, discovering that how you ask matters as much as what you ask.

modules.title: Draw Your Own Chatbot Brain
modules.sub: Create a picture that shows how a chatbot really thinks
modules.description: Learners design and draw a diagram or comic-style map of everything they now know about how a chatbot works, from reading examples to predicting words to making mistakes. They share their creation and explain one thing a chatbot would get wrong, showing they can teach the idea back in their own way.

synopsis: A curious kid stumbles into a behind-the-scenes tour of a chatbot factory, where the machines can talk but nobody has explained how â and it is your job to figure it out by running experiments, making choices, and fixing things when they go hilariously wrong. This wonder-driven adventure builds real intuition for how language models work through play, not explanation.
rationale: Young children are interacting with chatbots through toys, homework helpers, and voice assistants but have no mental model for what is actually happening, leaving them unable to form healthy expectations or notice when something has gone wrong.
learning_outcome: Students can explain in their own words that a chatbot predicts words based on patterns in text it has seen, demonstrate this by constructing a simple example, and identify one situation where a chatbot would likely give a wrong or silly answer.

nearest_existing: The Most Consequential Tool Built Without a Blueprint
